pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 23 (INSERT INTO demographic ... first_name='Maria')
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 23 (INSERT INTO demographic ... last_name='Garcia')
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 39 (dx_research_code='401') and ICD-9-CM documentation confirming code 401 = Essential Hypertension
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 39 (diagnosis_desc='Essential Hypertension') and web search confirming ICD-9-CM code 401 is Essential Hypertension
Code analysis: export_result.sh lines 15-17 (code='405') and ICD-9-CM documentation confirming code 405 = Secondary Hypertension
Code analysis: export_result.sh line 17 (diagnosis_desc LIKE '%Secondary Hypertension%') and web search confirming ICD-9-CM code 405 is Secondary Hypertension
Code analysis: setup_oscar.sh line 5 comment 'Default login: oscardoc', line 217 and 374 'Username: oscardoc'
Code analysis: setup_oscar.sh line 5 comment 'Default login: ... oscar', line 217 'password: oscar' and line 374 'Password: oscar'
Code analysis: setup_oscar.sh line 5 comment 'PIN: 1117', line 217 and line 375 'PIN: 1117'

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetic OSCAR EMR test environment with a test patient named Maria Garcia. The incorrect diagnosis code 401 (Essential Hypertension, confirmed via ICD-9-CM documentation) is seeded in the database and must be corrected to code 405 (Secondary Hypertension, confirmed via ICD-9-CM documentation). Login credentials are oscardoc/oscar/PIN:1117 as documented in the environment setup script. All diagnosis codes, patient names, and credentials have been independently verified against authoritative sources and code analysis.
